methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Vital Lyfe is a seed-stage consumer tech company founded by Jonathan Criss and Andrew Harner that has raised $24M to date. The comparable universe is sparse: one identified private peer (Matic Robotics) and no public comparables, limiting the breadth of triangulation available.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: stage-progression analysis. Using total capital raised ($24M) as a proxy for post-money valuation at seed close, application of a 2.5x multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $60M. This represents the median point in the triangulated range.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $51M to $69M, with $60M as the central estimate. Material uncertainties include the sparse comp set—particularly the absence of public consumer tech comparables and reliance on a single private peer—which constrains confidence in stage-progression assumptions. Additionally, the absence of disclosed metrics (revenue, user base, burn rate) prevents revenue multiple or efficiency-based cross-checks. Any subsequent valuation refinement would benefit from expanded comparable data and disclosure of operational metrics to support or revise the stage-progression assumption.
